title: Full Course Coverage Architecture, Experiment Registry & Locked Evaluation Plan

experiments.exercise_requirement: Inspect dimensions, grouped split composition, label balance, image values, text lengths, and representative examples.
experiments.research_question: What data properties and possible leakage risks must be understood before training?
experiments.hypothesis: The committed integrated dataset is class-balanced by construction and group-isolated, while image and text distributions reveal category-specific variation.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Build Keras datasets or NumPy inputs, inspect one batch, explain shapes, dtypes, labels, and shuffle policy.
experiments.research_question: Does the multimodal loader preserve image-text-label alignment and grouped split boundaries?
experiments.hypothesis: A deterministic loader with train-only shuffling will preserve aligned inputs and stable validation order.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Create a one-hidden-layer neural baseline, inspect weights, and confirm that repeated updates reduce loss on a small batch.
experiments.research_question: Can a minimal multimodal or single-modality neural model learn a valid gradient signal?
experiments.hypothesis: A compact baseline will reduce small-batch loss and produce finite class probabilities before full training.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Overfit a deliberately small batch and plot loss and accuracy learning curves.
experiments.research_question: Can the selected architecture memorize a small aligned batch, ruling out major implementation defects?
experiments.hypothesis: A sufficiently expressive model will approach near-perfect batch accuracy and very low loss.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Run controlled training and validation epochs, save learning curves, and document selected loop-failure demonstrations.
experiments.research_question: Does the training loop update only from train data while validation remains evaluation-only?
experiments.hypothesis: A correct loop will lower train loss while producing deterministic validation metrics and no validation updates.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Compare SGD, RMSprop, Adam, and AdamW across predefined learning rates; include schedule and early-stopping variants.
experiments.research_question: Which optimizer and learning-rate policy gives the best validation performance and stability under a fixed architecture?
experiments.hypothesis: Adaptive optimizers will converge faster on the compact dataset, while a controlled schedule may improve stability.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Compare small, medium, and large models, track probabilities and examples, and diagnose underfitting or overfitting.
experiments.research_question: How much capacity is justified by validation performance, stability, and efficiency?
experiments.hypothesis: The medium model will provide a better accuracy-efficiency balance than the largest model on the available data.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Compare regularization, batch normalization, learning-rate scheduling, skip connections, adapted pretrained features, and a convolutional alternative.
experiments.research_question: Which architecture-level techniques improve generalization without unnecessary complexity?
experiments.hypothesis: Moderate regularization and normalization will help stability, while excessive complexity will not consistently improve Macro F1.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Compare justified image scaling, resolution, feature extraction, and text length or vocabulary preprocessing variants.
experiments.research_question: Which preprocessing choices improve signal quality without introducing leakage or unnecessary dimensionality?
experiments.hypothesis: Task-aligned normalization and moderate resolution will outperform unscaled or unnecessarily large inputs.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Demonstrate failures from missing scaling, bad learning rates, excessive regularization, activation choice, misaligned labels, and vanishing gradients where feasible.
experiments.research_question: Can common training failures be reproduced safely and detected through objective diagnostics?
experiments.hypothesis: Each controlled fault will leave a distinct signature in loss, gradients, weights, timing, or alignment checks.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Run only in isolated diagnostic configurations.
Never train on validation data and never access the locked test split.
Misaligned labels are generated in memory from train-only copies and are not committed.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Inspect text features, lengths, vocabulary, label balance, and representative descriptions.
experiments.research_question: What linguistic patterns and sequence lengths characterize the automotive descriptions?
experiments.hypothesis: Descriptions will be short and domain-specific, with category terms and compatibility cues carrying substantial signal.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Prepare a deterministic text dataset and loader with train-only fitting and stable validation transformation.
experiments.research_question: Does the text pipeline avoid validation vocabulary leakage and preserve grouped labels?
experiments.hypothesis: Train-only vocabulary fitting and deterministic validation batching will pass leakage and alignment checks.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Lowercase and tokenize descriptions, reserve pad and unknown tokens, and display raw text, tokens, IDs, and padded sequences.
experiments.research_question: Does an explicit tokenizer produce reproducible sequences suitable for fair architecture comparisons?
experiments.hypothesis: A train-fitted word tokenizer with explicit special tokens will provide stable bounded-length inputs.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Train an embedding, average-pooling, dense classifier with multiclass cross-entropy.
experiments.research_question: How strong is the existing order-insensitive neural text baseline under the integrated split?
experiments.hypothesis: The dense embedding baseline will exceed majority performance but miss order-dependent cues.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Tune and compare a TF-IDF classical classifier against the dense neural baseline under the same grouped split.
experiments.research_question: Does sparse lexical modelling remain competitive with neural sequence representations on short domain text?
experiments.hypothesis: TF-IDF will remain a strong efficiency baseline but may generalize less well to paraphrased cues.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Train a Text CNN and a GRU; include an LSTM variant if the diagnostic budget permits, and compare speed and parameters.
experiments.research_question: Do local n-gram features or recurrent sequence state improve classification over average pooling?
experiments.hypothesis: Text CNN will be efficient on short descriptions, while GRU may better capture ordered compatibility phrases.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Build a small parameter-aware Transformer encoder and compare it fairly with dense, CNN, and recurrent models.
experiments.research_question: Can self-attention improve validation performance or error structure on short automotive descriptions?
experiments.hypothesis: A compact Transformer may improve phrase interactions but will not necessarily outperform simpler models on limited data.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_problem_title: Evaluate and compare all sequence models
experiments.exercise_requirement: Summarize train and validation curves, accuracy, Macro F1, confusion matrices, one-vs-rest ROC curves, examples, and cross-validation suitability.
experiments.research_question: Which sequence model offers the best evidence-based baseline after performance, complexity, and failure analysis?
experiments.hypothesis: The preferred model will be selected by validation Macro F1 with efficiency and stability tie-breakers, not by accuracy alone.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Return per-head attention weights and visualize at least two heads for correct and incorrect examples.
experiments.research_question: Do different attention heads focus on distinct tokens and plausible compatibility evidence?
experiments.hypothesis: Some heads will specialize in category or mismatch cues, while others may show diffuse or positional behaviour.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Adapt a compact pretrained transformer for classification and compare performance, attention, cost, and limitations.
experiments.research_question: Does transfer learning improve representation quality enough to justify additional dependencies and compute?
experiments.hypothesis: A compact pretrained encoder may improve robustness, but gains may be limited by the small domain-specific dataset.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
prerequisites: Explicit approval before downloading pretrained weights.
Record model license and exact revision.
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Inspect image dimensions, categories, labels, diversity, difficult examples, annotation inconsistencies, and build a Keras dataset.
experiments.research_question: What visual diversity, category balance, and annotation ambiguity must the vision experiments account for?
experiments.hypothesis: The open-license dataset is balanced by label construction but contains category-specific backgrounds, scales, and fine-detail challenges.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Compare two or three frozen backbones, including convolutional and transformer families, with constant splits, resizing, metrics, and dense heads.
experiments.research_question: Which frozen pretrained representation best transfers to automotive-part classification and multimodal matching?
experiments.hypothesis: A modern convolutional backbone will provide strong efficiency, while a compact vision transformer may capture broader shape context.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
prerequisites: Explicit approval before downloading pretrained weights.
Record model license and exact weight revision.
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Learn image-text compatibility scores, compare at least two equal-pair strategies, and test reversed-pair antisymmetry.
experiments.research_question: Can the three-class relation task be represented as an ordered compatibility score with stable pairwise differences?
experiments.hypothesis: MATCH descriptions will receive higher scores than PARTIAL_MATCH and MISMATCH, with flip consistency near antisymmetry.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Compare final and intermediate convolutional features, transformer token pooling choices, and two or three image resolutions.
experiments.research_question: Which representation layer and resolution preserve useful part detail without excessive compute?
experiments.hypothesis: Intermediate or multi-scale features may help fine details, while higher resolution will benefit only selected categories.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Use at least one model-independent method such as occlusion, masking, crop-and-retest, or perturbation to identify influential regions.
experiments.research_question: Do image models rely on plausible part regions rather than backgrounds or collection shortcuts?
experiments.hypothesis: Correct predictions will depend on housings, connectors, openings, filters, lenses, or mounting details rather than background alone.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Gradually compare frozen features, trainable head, final block, final third, and limited full fine-tuning with controlled optimization.
experiments.research_question: How much validation improvement justifies unfreezing additional pretrained parameters?
experiments.hypothesis: Partial fine-tuning may improve category-sensitive features, but full fine-tuning will overfit unless tightly controlled.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
prerequisites: VIS-002 champion frozen backbone selected on validation only.
Explicit operator approval for Tier 4 execution.
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Collect a genuine independently annotated subset and compare filtering, agreement weighting, confidence weighting, and soft targets.
experiments.research_question: How do label confidence and inter-annotator agreement affect calibration and performance on clear versus ambiguous examples?
experiments.hypothesis: Agreement-aware training may improve calibration on ambiguous PARTIAL_MATCH cases without inflating clear-case accuracy.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
prerequisites: At least two real independent human annotators.
Do not infer or fabricate agreement from existing single-author labels.
safety_notes: Human agreement metrics must be based on genuine independent annotations.
Do not simulate annotators or report synthetic agreement as human evidence.
Train and validation evidence only; test remains locked.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Identify real failures, test augmentations individually, document label validity, then evaluate a justified combined pipeline.
experiments.research_question: Which label-preserving transformations improve robustness to observed capture variation without damaging fine detail?
experiments.hypothesis: Moderate crop, exposure, compression, and background variation will help targeted failures, while aggressive transforms may harm detail-sensitive categories.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.

experiments.exercise_requirement: Measure pairwise consistency, flip consistency, transitivity, equal-pair accuracy, agreement-stratified performance, parameters, and inference time.
experiments.research_question: Do compatibility scores form a coherent ordering beyond ordinary three-class accuracy?
experiments.hypothesis: A useful scorer will preserve pair reversal and most ordering triples, while ambiguous partial matches will dominate cycles and low margins.
experiments.random_seed_policy: primary seed 42; retained comparisons repeat seeds 42, 43, and 44
safety_notes: Train and validation evidence only.
Do not load, inspect, score, or tune against the locked test split.
